text:
Once upon an age-old oak tree lived a wise old fox who wore spectacles. Every morning, before dawn's first light, he'd climb down from his lofty perch and stroll through the forest. Along the way, he’d listen to the whispering leaves and watch squirrels scamper.

One misty morning, the fox spotted something peculiar—a beautiful young doe weaving through the shadows. Not just any doe, but an enchanted healer, rumored to soothe wild things. The fox felt a curious pull and began quietly approaching.

The doe stopped. She gave him a soft gaze that seemed to pierce his fox soul.

“Good morrow,” she murmured.

“The stars speak secrets,” began the fox politely.

She licked her paw. “What secrets?”

“Our forest lives.”

“A tale?”

“Yes.”

“Wealth,” she said slowly.

“Fawns.”

“You have many.”

“Just one.”

“You keep it hidden?”

“Yes.”

“What is it?”

“A thicket.”
